title: Surface blob fetch failures to client
description: server/server.ts:105-126 — when blob API fails and cache is empty, server returns an empty list. Client shows 'no images' instead of 'couldn't load'. Return 503 (or pass through 429) so the UI can render an error state and offer retry.

title: Require explicit campaign id in campaigns.yaml
description: server/server.ts:135 falls back to Math.random().toString(36) when neither hashtag nor icon_path is present. Non-deterministic slugs break hash-URL deep linking across reloads. Require an explicit 'id' field in data/campaigns.yaml and error at startup if missing.

title: Add TTL and size cap to server content cache
description: server/server.ts:85 — contentCache.set() has no TTL or size limit. Memory grows unbounded and stale content is never invalidated. Reuse the 30s-TTL pattern already used for blob listings; cap entry count.

title: Gate BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN log behind IS_DEV
description: server/server.ts:63 logs token presence unconditionally. Safe in dev, risky if prod logs are exposed. Only log presence/absence when IS_DEV is true.

title: Add timeout to image preload
description: client/src/App.tsx:169-184 — Image.src has no timeout, so a slow/dead CDN response freezes the loading state indefinitely. Wrap each preload in Promise.race with a timeout and surface a recoverable error in the UI.

title: Fix content modal load race
description: client/src/App.tsx:269-303 — activeModalRef check is fragile under slow network. If user opens modal A, then B before A loads, A's content can clobber B. Use AbortController or a version counter pattern (same fix shape as the campaign-load race).

title: Fix race condition in campaign image loading
description: client/src/App.tsx:123-210 — rapid campaign switching can resolve stale promises into state because isCampaignLoadingRef only gates new loads but doesn't cancel in-flight ones. Add AbortController or version token so only the latest selection's results are applied.

title: Add React error boundary around app shell
description: Currently any uncaught render error crashes the entire gallery (client/src/App.tsx). Add an ErrorBoundary component with a graceful fallback UI and wrap the top-level tree.

title: Split App.tsx into focused components
description: Extract from client/src/App.tsx (~1100 lines): <CampaignNavigation>, <LightboxContainer>, <ContentModal>, <LoadingManager>. Co-locate related state. Reduces stale-closure risk and clarifies ownership of state.

title: Epic: Server hardening
description: server/server.ts has several risky patterns: token presence logged unconditionally, unbounded content cache, non-deterministic campaign slug fallback, swallowed blob errors, handrolled .env parser, no input validation. Tighten the production code path.

title: Epic: Decompose monolithic App.tsx
description: App.tsx is ~1100 lines with 20+ useState calls, mixing toolbar, sidebar, lightbox, modals, settings, campaign selection, and loading screen logic. This causes stale-closure bugs, race conditions, and makes the code hard to reason about. Break into smaller components and add an error boundary.

title: Detect collisions when deriving campaign ids
description: server/server.ts:158-172 deriveCampaignId produces one slug per campaign but does not check for duplicates across campaigns. Two campaigns with the same hashtag or icon_path would silently collapse to the same id; subsequent find-by-id lookups (e.g. /api/campaigns/:id/images) would always return the first one, making the second unreachable via deep link. Current data has no collisions (all entries use unique icon_path values), but a future yaml edit could introduce one silently. After mapping, scan for duplicate ids and throw at load time with the offending hashtag/icon_path in the error message. Surfaced in PR 40 review.

title: Extract useFocusTrap hook shared by ContentModal and settings modal
description: ContentModal.tsx:29-71 and App.tsx:490-529 (settings modal effect) are near-duplicates: same Escape handling, same Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, same querySelectorAll selector list. Extract into a useFocusTrap(active, modalRef, onClose) hook in client/src/hooks/ and use it in both places. Reduces drift risk if focusable-selector list or escape semantics ever change.

title: Resolve disabled-vs-abort inconsistency in campaign switch
description: App.tsx disables Home/Campaigns/nav arrows/sidebar items when isCampaignLoading is true (App.tsx:548-616), which prevents users from triggering the abort-on-switch logic added in fringematrix5-2du. The PR description claims 'the latest user action wins' via AbortController, but the disabled state blocks that path for non-cached loads. Pick one: drop disabled and rely on abort, OR keep disabled and remove the now-dead abort handling for user-driven switches (the abort still has value for the mount-load -> first-user-click overlap). Surfaced in PR 39 follow-up review.

title: Test: error boundary fallback
description: Once the React error boundary exists, add a client test that throws inside a child and asserts the fallback renders instead of an unmounted tree.

title: Test: modal focus-trap contract
description: The focus management contract documented in client/src/App.tsx:52-62 (tab trap, close-button focus restoration) has no automated test. Add a Playwright or @testing-library/user-event test verifying tab cycles within the modal and focus returns to the trigger on close.

title: Test: malformed campaigns.yaml handling
description: Add a server test that loads a malformed/missing data/campaigns.yaml and asserts the server fails fast with a clear error rather than serving partial/garbage data.

title: Test: blob API failure paths
description: Add server tests that simulate Vercel Blob returning errors (rate limit, 5xx, network failure) and assert the API responds correctly (proper status code, retry-after honored) — see server/server.ts:105-126.
close_reason: Addressed by PR 40: api.blob-errors.test.ts covers rate-limit (429+Retry-After), connection error (503), retry-with-non-rate-limit-error (503), and retry-success caching. Residual coverage gaps (avatars/* route, expired-cache-on-rate-limit) filed separately.

title: Replace handrolled .env.local parser with dotenv
description: server/server.ts:41-64 implements its own regex-based .env parser. Fails on escaped quotes, multiline values, and gives no type coercion. Switch to the dotenv package (likely already transitive).

title: Epic: Test coverage gaps
description: Tests cover happy paths but miss error scenarios: blob API failures, malformed campaigns.yaml, modal focus-trap contract documented in App.tsx, and error boundary fallback. Add coverage for failure modes.

title: Reconsider 'No token found' string-match fallback in listBlobsWithCache
description: server/server.ts:158-161 catches errors whose message includes 'No token found' and returns an empty blob list. The HAS_BLOB_TOKEN check at line 111 already handles the legitimate fallback case (token unset). The string-match exists for the scenario where BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN is set but invalid, but treating that as 'empty list' silently hides real config errors in production. Consider removing the string match so an invalid token surfaces as 503 BlobUnavailableError instead. Validate with a deliberate-invalid-token integration test before changing.

title: Test coverage: content cache TTL and eviction
description: server/server.ts:358-383 implements TTL (5 min) and a size-cap eviction policy (CONTENT_CACHE_MAX = 16, evicts oldest by insertion order). Neither path has a test. With only 3 valid content pages today the eviction branch is unreachable in practice, but future expansion of VALID_CONTENT_PAGES would silently regress the policy if untested. Add tests that: (1) cache hit returns within TTL, (2) cache miss after TTL re-reads file, (3) eviction removes oldest entry when capacity is exceeded. May require Date.now() mocking or a smaller CONTENT_CACHE_MAX via dependency injection.

title: Test coverage: /avatars/* route
description: The /avatars/* redirect route (server/server.ts:233-263) is entirely uncovered by tests. PR 40 added BlobUnavailableError -> 503 mapping to this route but no test exercises it. Add at least: (1) 302 redirect on found blob, (2) 404 when blob not present, (3) 503 when Vercel Blob is unavailable. Use the same jest.unstable_mockModule pattern from api.blob-errors.test.ts.

title: Document or fix mount-effect empty deps with loadCampaignImages
description: App.tsx mount effect (lines 396-441) calls loadCampaignImages from inside an async IIFE but declares deps as []. loadCampaignImages is a stable useCallback([]) so behavior is correct, but react-hooks/exhaustive-deps would flag it. Either add a one-line comment explaining the intentional empty deps, or include loadCampaignImages in the array (safe because it's stable). Pre-emptive cleanup before any future lint config change.

title: Decide fate of activeModalRef now that openModal uses AbortController
description: openModal in App.tsx aborts the prior controller and checks signal.aborted at every await boundary. The activeModalRef.current === page guards on lines 308/316/320 are mostly redundant with that abort check. Comment calls them 'defense in depth'. Either remove activeModalRef, OR add a clarifying comment explaining the one branch it covers that the signal does not (state mutation in a non-aborted-but-superseded scenario, if any actually exists). Surfaced in PR 39 follow-up review.

title: Guard localStorage access for SSR safety
description: client/src/App.tsx:89, 104 access localStorage in effects without a typeof window guard. Not a bug today (CSR-only) but blocks adding SSR. Use lazy init with the typeof window check.

title: Replace catch (error: any) with typed errors
description: server/server.ts uses 'catch (error: any)' throughout, accessing .message/.name/.retryAfter without guards. Introduce a small typed error hierarchy (BlobRateLimitError, BlobUnavailableError, ...) so retry/backoff logic is auditable.

title: Enable TypeScript strict mode
description: Both client/tsconfig.json and server/tsconfig.json are not in strict mode; 'any' types appear in server.ts and App.tsx. Enable strict: true incrementally (start with noImplicitAny + strictNullChecks) and fix fallout.

title: Extract magic numbers to named constants
description: client/src/App.tsx:727 (swipe thresholds 50px/75px/500ms) and client/src/hooks/useLightboxAnimations.ts:27-30 (retry frames, 500ms timeout) use unexplained literals. Pull into named constants with a one-line comment explaining the choice.

title: Add pagination for blob listings
description: server/server.ts:234 uses limit: 1000 for blob listing. Fine today, but campaigns with thousands of images will OOM the response. Add cursor pagination on the server endpoint and stream/page on the client.

title: Archive or update stale top-level docs
description: DEPLOYMENT_STATUS.md and VERCEL_BUILD_DEBUG.md look like migration-era debug artifacts referencing old 401/protection issues. Either update to current state or move to docs/archive/ (or delete).

title: Gitignore ai_logs/ and remove from repo
description: ai_logs/ contains ~2.3 MB of Cursor conversation logs checked into the repo. Bloats deploys and clones. Add to .gitignore and remove via git rm.

title: Epic: Repo hygiene & polish
description: Cleanup of stale docs, checked-in logs, magic numbers, loose TypeScript config, and hydration guards. Lower-priority quality-of-life improvements.

title: Clear modalLoadAbortRef.current after abort in closeModal
description: closeModal (App.tsx:328-340) calls modalLoadAbortRef.current?.abort() but leaves the ref pointing at the aborted controller. Functionally harmless (GC'd next openModal call), but inconsistent with the pattern elsewhere and a minor code-smell. Set the ref to null after abort to match.
